expected_discriminator.inconclusive: The observer cannot collect classifiable same-boot stack/callsite evidence or codeword slide acceptance is unavailable.
expected_discriminator.negative: No worker execution context/callsite intersects the firmware_class/qcacld-HDD stack in the captured window, pushing the tail away from workqueue execution.
expected_discriminator.positive: The execute_start call-stack/callsite oracle links at least one worker context to the V2253 firmware_class/qcacld-HDD stack or a source-backed wrapper that reaches it.

next_runner_contract: Do not flash or run another identical combined workqueue/codeword capture for V2275/V2276 evidence.
Design/build a focused workqueue execute_start call-stack or callsite observer that records worker execution context around the firmware_class/qcacld-HDD tail.
Use a same-boot codeword slide only with the bounded UAO-patch-aware acceptance rule proven in V2276.
Keep raw stack/callsite samples private; publish only redacted metadata and target-hit summaries.
If a live run is needed, use the normal rollbackable boot-only flash gates and avoid Wi-Fi scan/connect/DHCP/ping unless the new oracle explicitly requires it.
candidates.summary: V2276 made the V2275 workqueue function-pointer evidence classifiable and negative; the next independent T1 unit should observe workqueue execute_start call-stack/callsite context rather than work->func alone.

v2276_result.pc_mismatch_explanation: all three PC mismatches are ARM64 UAO runtime alternatives

why_independent: It does not rerun generic CPU-clock sampling.
It does not repeat the completed V2274/V2275 work->func oracle.
It targets worker execution context/callsite identity, which can explain why V2253 saw the firmware_class/qcacld-HDD stack while work->func had no target hits.
